name: Aetna (CVS Health)
overview: Aetna, a CVS Health company, is one of the nation's leading diversified healthcare benefits companies. With roots dating back to 1853, Aetna serves approximately 3.7 million Medicare Advantage members across 46 states. The 2018 acquisition by CVS Health created a vertically integrated healthcare company combining insurance, pharmacy benefits, and retail health services.
medicarePlans: Aetna offers a comprehensive range of Medicare products including Medicare Advantage HMO and PPO plans, stand-alone Part D prescription drug plans, and Medicare Supplement (Medigap) policies. Many Aetna Medicare Advantage plans feature $0 monthly premiums, dental, vision, and hearing benefits, fitness programs like SilverSneakers, and over-the-counter allowances.
coverageAreas: Aetna Medicare Advantage plans are available in 46 states and the District of Columbia. Coverage is particularly strong in the Northeast, Southeast, and major metropolitan areas across the country. Their PPO plans tend to offer broader network flexibility compared to HMO options.

name: Anthem Blue Cross (Elevance Health)
strengths: Blue Cross Blue Shield brand recognition
overview: Anthem Blue Cross, operating under the Elevance Health (formerly Anthem Inc.) umbrella, is a major health insurance provider and the largest for-profit managed care company in the Blue Cross Blue Shield Association. The company serves over 2.1 million Medicare Advantage members across 14 states where it operates under the Anthem, Empire, or Wellpoint brands.
medicarePlans: Anthem offers Medicare Advantage HMO and PPO plans, including dual-eligible Special Needs Plans (D-SNPs). Their plans frequently include $0 premium options, dental and vision coverage, prescription drug benefits, fitness programs, and telehealth services. They also offer stand-alone Part D and Medigap supplemental policies.
coverageAreas: Anthem operates in 14 states including California, Colorado, Connecticut, Georgia, Indiana, Kentucky, Maine, Missouri, Nevada, New Hampshire, New York, Ohio, Virginia, and Wisconsin. Network strength varies significantly by market.

name: Blue Cross Blue Shield
weaknesses: Can be more expensive than competitors
overview: Blue Cross Blue Shield is a federation of 34 separate health insurance companies that provide coverage across all 50 states. Combined, BCBS companies cover more than 115 million Americans, making it the largest health insurance brand in the country. Each BCBS company operates independently, so plan options, premiums, and quality ratings vary by state.
medicarePlans: BCBS companies collectively offer one of the broadest arrays of Medicare products in the country, including Medicare Advantage HMO, PPO, and PFFS plans, Medicare Supplement policies (Plans A through N), and Part D prescription drug plans. Many BCBS Medicare Advantage plans include additional benefits like dental, vision, hearing, and wellness programs.
coverageAreas: Blue Cross Blue Shield plans are available in all 50 states and the District of Columbia through the federation of local BCBS companies. The BlueCard program allows members to access care from BCBS providers nationwide, even when traveling outside their home service area.

name: Cigna Healthcare
weaknesses: Limited plan availability in some states
overview: Cigna Healthcare, part of The Cigna Group, provides Medicare Advantage plans in approximately 30 states. While Cigna is better known for its commercial and employer-sponsored health plans, the company has been expanding its Medicare presence in recent years, serving approximately 730,000 Medicare Advantage members.
medicarePlans: Cigna offers Medicare Advantage HMO and PPO plans with benefits that often include $0 premium options, prescription drug coverage, dental and vision benefits, virtual care access, and fitness programs. They also provide stand-alone Part D plans through their Express Scripts pharmacy benefit management subsidiary.
coverageAreas: Cigna Medicare Advantage plans are available in approximately 30 states, with the strongest presence in the South, Southwest, and select metropolitan markets. Their Part D plans through Express Scripts have broader national availability.

name: Humana Inc.
strengths: High star ratings on many plans
weaknesses: Limited PPO options in some markets
overview: Humana is the second-largest Medicare Advantage insurer in the United States, serving approximately 5.4 million MA members across 49 states. Founded in 1961 in Louisville, Kentucky, Humana has evolved from a nursing home company into one of the nation's most Medicare-focused health insurers, with Medicare representing the majority of its revenue.
medicarePlans: Humana offers an extensive lineup of Medicare products including Medicare Advantage HMO, PPO, and PFFS plans, stand-alone Part D prescription drug plans, Medicare Supplement policies, and dual-eligible Special Needs Plans. Many plans feature $0 premiums, comprehensive dental/vision/hearing benefits, over-the-counter allowances, meal delivery, and transportation assistance.
coverageAreas: Humana Medicare Advantage plans are available in 49 states and the District of Columbia, with particular strength in Florida, Texas, Kentucky, Ohio, and throughout the Southeast. Humana's CenterWell Senior Primary Care clinics provide dedicated senior-focused primary care in select markets.

name: UnitedHealthcare (UnitedHealth Group)
weaknesses: Large size can mean less personalized service
overview: UnitedHealthcare is the largest Medicare Advantage insurer in the United States, serving approximately 7.8 million MA members across all 50 states. As a subsidiary of UnitedHealth Group, UnitedHealthcare benefits from the integrated Optum health services platform, which includes primary care delivery, pharmacy benefits, and data analytics.
medicarePlans: UnitedHealthcare offers the broadest range of Medicare products in the nation, including AARP-branded Medicare Advantage and Supplement plans, HMO and PPO Medicare Advantage options, stand-alone Part D plans, Special Needs Plans, and institutional plans. Benefits frequently include $0 premiums, dental/vision/hearing, fitness programs (Renew Active), and extensive over-the-counter allowances.
coverageAreas: UnitedHealthcare Medicare Advantage plans are available in all 50 states and the District of Columbia, making it the most geographically accessible Medicare Advantage insurer. Their AARP Medicare Supplement plans are available nationwide.

name: Kaiser Permanente
weaknesses: Must use Kaiser facilities and doctors
overview: Kaiser Permanente is a unique integrated managed care consortium operating in 8 states and the District of Columbia. Unlike traditional insurers, Kaiser operates its own hospitals, medical offices, and employs its own physicians. This integrated model consistently earns Kaiser some of the highest CMS star ratings in the Medicare Advantage program, serving approximately 1.8 million Medicare members.
medicarePlans: Kaiser Permanente offers Medicare Advantage HMO plans that combine medical coverage, prescription drugs, and additional benefits into one integrated plan. Benefits typically include $0 or low monthly premiums, comprehensive prescription drug coverage, extensive preventive care, mental health services, and wellness programs. Kaiser's integrated electronic health record system ensures coordinated care across all providers.
coverageAreas: Kaiser Permanente Medicare plans are available in California, Colorado, Georgia, Hawaii, Maryland, Oregon, Virginia, Washington, and the District of Columbia. Coverage requires using Kaiser Permanente facilities and physicians, with limited out-of-network coverage for emergency and urgent care.

name: Molina Healthcare
overview: Molina Healthcare specializes in government-sponsored healthcare, with a particular focus on serving low-income and dual-eligible Medicare-Medicaid beneficiaries. Founded by Dr. C. David Molina in 1980 to provide care to underserved communities, Molina now serves approximately 215,000 Medicare members across 15 states.
medicarePlans: Molina offers Medicare Advantage HMO plans and Dual-Eligible Special Needs Plans (D-SNPs) designed for beneficiaries who qualify for both Medicare and Medicaid. Their plans typically feature very low or $0 premiums, integrated prescription drug coverage, dental and vision benefits, transportation assistance, and care coordination services tailored to complex medical needs.
coverageAreas: Molina Healthcare Medicare plans are available in select counties across approximately 15 states including California, Florida, Illinois, Michigan, New York, Ohio, South Carolina, Texas, Washington, and Wisconsin.

name: Centene Corporation (WellCare)
overview: Centene Corporation is one of the largest managed care organizations in the country, operating Medicare Advantage plans primarily through its WellCare subsidiary. Following the 2020 merger with WellCare, Centene serves approximately 2.8 million Medicare Advantage members across 38 states, making it the third-largest MA insurer.
medicarePlans: Through WellCare and its other brands, Centene offers Medicare Advantage HMO and PPO plans, stand-alone Part D prescription drug plans, and Dual-Eligible Special Needs Plans. Many plans include $0 premiums, dental and vision benefits, over-the-counter allowances, transportation, meal delivery, and fitness programs.
coverageAreas: Centene/WellCare Medicare Advantage plans are available in 38 states with strength in Florida, Georgia, New York, Texas, and throughout the Southeast. Their rural market penetration is among the best in the industry.

name: WellCare Health Plans
strengths: Strong presence in Florida and Southeast
overview: WellCare Health Plans, now a subsidiary of Centene Corporation, has been providing Medicare Advantage plans since 1985. Originally headquartered in Tampa, Florida, WellCare serves approximately 1.6 million Medicare members across 32 states. The company has traditionally focused on government-sponsored healthcare programs and maintains a particularly strong presence in the Southeast.
medicarePlans: WellCare offers Medicare Advantage HMO and PPO plans, Part D prescription drug plans, and Special Needs Plans for dual-eligible beneficiaries. Plans commonly feature $0 monthly premiums, dental and vision benefits, hearing coverage, over-the-counter quarterly allowances, transportation to medical appointments, and SilverSneakers fitness memberships.
coverageAreas: WellCare Medicare plans are available in 32 states, with particular strength in Florida (where the company was founded), Georgia, New York, Texas, Kentucky, and other Southeastern states.

name: Mutual of Omaha
weaknesses: Does not offer Medicare Advantage plans
Limited additional benefits compared to MA plans
overview: Mutual of Omaha is one of the largest and most recognized Medicare Supplement (Medigap) insurance providers in the United States. Founded in 1909, this mutual insurance company has built a strong reputation for financial stability and competitive Medigap pricing. While they do not offer Medicare Advantage plans, they are a leading choice for beneficiaries who prefer Original Medicare with supplemental coverage.
medicarePlans: Mutual of Omaha specializes exclusively in Medicare Supplement (Medigap) policies, offering Plans A, B, F (where available), G, and N. Their Plan G has been particularly popular as the most comprehensive Medigap option for those newly eligible for Medicare. They offer competitive premiums, a household discount for married couples, and accept applicants during their Medigap Open Enrollment Period.
coverageAreas: Mutual of Omaha Medicare Supplement plans are available in all 50 states and the District of Columbia. Because Medigap plans work with Original Medicare, there are no provider network restrictions — beneficiaries can see any doctor or specialist who accepts Medicare.

name: AARP/UnitedHealthcare Medicare
weaknesses: Can be more expensive than non-AARP alternatives
AARP membership required for some benefits
overview: AARP Medicare plans are offered by UnitedHealthcare through an exclusive partnership with AARP, the nation's largest organization for people age 50 and older. This partnership combines AARP's trusted brand and advocacy mission with UnitedHealthcare's health plan infrastructure. Together, they serve approximately 4.2 million Medicare members nationwide.
medicarePlans: AARP/UnitedHealthcare offers a full suite of Medicare products including AARP Medicare Advantage HMO and PPO plans, AARP Medicare Supplement Plans (the most popular Medigap brand in America), and AARP Medicare Rx prescription drug plans through a Walgreens co-branded pharmacy network. Plans include benefits like Renew Active fitness, dental, vision, hearing, and telehealth.
coverageAreas: AARP/UnitedHealthcare Medicare plans are available in all 50 states and the District of Columbia. AARP Medicare Supplement plans have particularly broad availability, while Medicare Advantage plan options vary by county.

name: SilverScript (CVS Health)
strengths: Multiple plan tiers for different budgets
weaknesses: Part D only — no MA or Medigap
overview: SilverScript, a CVS Health company, is the largest stand-alone Medicare Part D prescription drug plan provider in the United States. Operating as part of the Aetna/CVS Health family, SilverScript provides prescription drug coverage to millions of Medicare beneficiaries across all 50 states through their partnership with CVS Pharmacy's extensive retail network.
medicarePlans: SilverScript offers stand-alone Medicare Part D prescription drug plans at multiple coverage levels to fit different budgets and medication needs. Plans range from low-premium basic coverage to more comprehensive options with lower copays and broader formularies. All plans leverage the CVS Pharmacy network, including mail-order pharmacy services and CVS MinuteClinic access.
coverageAreas: SilverScript Medicare Part D plans are available in all 50 states and the District of Columbia. The plans work with Original Medicare or Medicare Supplement policies for beneficiaries who want separate prescription drug coverage.

name: Devoted Health
weaknesses: Newer company with shorter track record
No Medigap or Part D standalone plans
overview: Devoted Health is a technology-driven Medicare Advantage company founded in 2017 by former Athenahealth executives. Despite being relatively new, Devoted has earned strong CMS star ratings and high member satisfaction scores. The company assigns each member a dedicated Devoted Guide who helps coordinate care, manage benefits, and navigate the healthcare system.
medicarePlans: Devoted Health offers Medicare Advantage HMO and PPO plans that combine medical, prescription drug, and supplemental benefits. Plans typically feature $0 premiums, comprehensive dental and vision coverage, hearing benefits, over-the-counter allowances, transportation, meals after hospital stays, and personal care guide support. Their technology platform focuses on proactive care coordination.
coverageAreas: Devoted Health is available in select counties across 12 states including Florida, Texas, Ohio, Arizona, Illinois, Alabama, Georgia, Oklahoma, South Carolina, Tennessee, Virginia, and Indiana. The company continues to expand its geographic footprint annually.

name: Clover Health
overview: Clover Health is a technology company using its proprietary Clover Assistant platform to improve clinical decision-making for Medicare Advantage members. Founded in 2014 and publicly traded since 2021, Clover takes a unique approach by focusing on empowering physicians with AI-driven clinical insights rather than building traditional insurance infrastructure.
medicarePlans: Clover Health offers Medicare Advantage HMO and PPO plans with integrated Part D prescription drug coverage. Plans commonly include $0 premiums, dental and vision benefits, hearing coverage, over-the-counter allowances, fitness programs, and telehealth access. Their PPO plans provide flexibility to see out-of-network providers at higher cost-sharing.
coverageAreas: Clover Health Medicare Advantage plans are available in select counties across 8 states including New Jersey, Georgia, Arizona, South Carolina, Mississippi, Tennessee, Texas, and Pennsylvania.

name: Oscar Health
overview: Oscar Health is a technology-focused health insurance company that entered the Medicare Advantage market to bring its modern, user-friendly approach to senior healthcare. Known for its intuitive mobile app and emphasis on technology-driven care, Oscar serves approximately 42,000 Medicare Advantage members in a small but growing number of markets.
medicarePlans: Oscar Health offers Medicare Advantage HMO plans featuring an integrated technology platform, dedicated concierge care team, seamless telehealth access, prescription drug coverage, and supplemental benefits including dental, vision, and hearing. Their digital-first approach includes an easy-to-use app for managing benefits, finding providers, and accessing care.
coverageAreas: Oscar Health Medicare Advantage plans are currently available in select counties across approximately 5 states including New York, Texas, Florida, Arizona, and California. The company has been selectively expanding its Medicare footprint.

name: Bright Health
overview: Bright Health is a health insurance company that takes a focused partnership approach by building deep relationships with select health system partners in each market. The company has faced financial challenges and has significantly reduced its market presence in recent years, but continues to serve Medicare Advantage members in select markets with its integrated care model.
medicarePlans: Bright Health offers Medicare Advantage HMO plans built around partnerships with leading local health systems. Plans may include $0 premiums, prescription drug coverage, dental and vision benefits, and care coordination through their partner health systems. The integrated model aims to improve care quality through alignment between insurer and provider.
coverageAreas: Bright Health has significantly reduced its Medicare Advantage footprint and is currently available in select counties across approximately 4 states. Prospective members should verify current availability in their area.

name: Alignment Healthcare
strengths: AVA technology platform for care management
weaknesses: Very limited to California, Nevada, North Carolina, Arizona
overview: Alignment Healthcare is a tech-enabled Medicare Advantage company focused on transforming healthcare for seniors through its proprietary AVA clinical intelligence platform. Founded by healthcare industry veterans, Alignment serves approximately 115,000 Medicare Advantage members primarily in California, with expansion into select markets in Nevada, North Carolina, and Arizona.
medicarePlans: Alignment Healthcare offers Medicare Advantage HMO plans designed around their AVA platform, which uses data analytics to proactively manage member health. Plans typically include $0 premiums, comprehensive dental, vision, and hearing benefits, prescription drug coverage, transportation, meals, fitness programs, and 24/7 nurse hotline access.
coverageAreas: Alignment Healthcare Medicare Advantage plans are available in select counties across California (their primary market), Nevada, North Carolina, and Arizona. The company continues to expand but maintains a selective, quality-focused growth strategy.

name: SCAN Health Plan
strengths: Focus on senior health and wellness
weaknesses: Limited to California, Arizona, Nevada, and Texas
overview: SCAN Health Plan is a nonprofit Medicare Advantage plan that has been dedicated to keeping seniors healthy and independent since 1977. One of the nation's largest not-for-profit Medicare Advantage plans, SCAN serves approximately 280,000 members primarily in California. SCAN consistently earns high CMS star ratings, reflecting its commitment to quality care and member satisfaction.
medicarePlans: SCAN Health Plan offers Medicare Advantage HMO plans and stand-alone Part D prescription drug plans. Their MA plans typically feature $0 monthly premiums, comprehensive prescription drug coverage, dental, vision, and hearing benefits, SilverSneakers fitness, over-the-counter allowances, transportation, and their signature Independence at Home program for members who need additional support.
coverageAreas: SCAN Health Plan is available in select counties across California (their primary market), Arizona, Nevada, and Texas. The company has been expanding beyond California but maintains a focused growth approach to preserve quality.

name: AgeWell New York
strengths: Deep knowledge of New York market
weaknesses: Only available in New York City area
Less financial resources than large insurers
overview: AgeWell New York is a managed care organization specializing in Medicare Advantage and Managed Long-Term Care plans for seniors in the New York City metropolitan area. Founded in 2012, AgeWell focuses on serving diverse communities throughout New York's five boroughs and surrounding counties with culturally sensitive, community-based healthcare services.
medicarePlans: AgeWell offers Medicare Advantage HMO plans and dual-eligible Special Needs Plans designed for New York City area seniors. Plans include medical coverage, prescription drugs, dental and vision benefits, transportation, over-the-counter allowances, and care coordination. Their multilingual staff provides services in English, Spanish, Chinese, Russian, and other languages spoken by New York's diverse senior population.
coverageAreas: AgeWell New York is available exclusively in the New York City metropolitan area, including counties in the five boroughs (Manhattan, Brooklyn, Queens, Bronx, Staten Island) and select surrounding counties in the greater New York area.
